key_departures.impact: Team MVP by one account, posted 1,023 scrimmage yards and 8 TDs in 2025 on a 1-year prove-it deal. Was both a rushing and receiving threat. Departure leaves a clear RB1 vacancy.

Three-year starting LG in Pittsburgh. 7th-best pass-blocking grade among guards in 2025 (PFF 76.4). Departure creates a significant hole at left guard with no obvious in-house replacement beyond Spencer Anderson.
Stepped into starting role mid-2025 season after Darius Slay departure. Solid veteran CB depth piece. Loss partially mitigated by re-signing of Samuel Jr. and signing of Dean.

WR2 by default in 2025 with only 31 catches for 372 yards in 14 games. Limited production made his departure low-impact; need at WR2 existed before his departure and was immediately addressed via the Pittman trade.
Disaster in his lone Pittsburgh season — 5.8 yds/rec, 4.1 yds/target. Release saves ~$7M in cap space. With Freiermuth and Darnell Washington on roster, impact on TE need is minimal.
Versatile TE/FB hybrid and core special teamer. Four-year run in Pittsburgh ends. Adds mild depth need at TE/FB hybrid role, though not a starter.
Was a starting safety option in 2025 but is now unsigned. Departure contributed to heavy safety investment in free agency (Savage + Brisker added).
Depth safety now unsigned. Contributes to the safety room turnover that prompted the Savage and Brisker additions.
Rotational safety now unsigned. Part of wholesale secondary turnover at the position.
Veteran depth WR retired. Low production role; departure barely registers given Pittman acquisition.
Former backup/bridge QB. Will mentor Cam Ward in Tennessee. Departure leaves Mason Rudolph and Will Howard as backup options with Aaron Rodgers' return uncertain.
Core special teamer loss. Minor roster impact.
Won the punting job in 2025 (45.5 avg), now a free agent. Replaced by Cameron Johnston re-signing.

signings.trade_cost: PIT 6th-round pick for MPJ + IND 7th-round pick
signings.analysis: Three-year, $59M extension upon acquisition makes this a genuine long-term commitment at WR2. Pittman had 80 catches, 784 yards, 7 TDs in 2025 and has posted at least 65 catches every full season. Pairs with DK Metcalf to give Pittsburgh a legitimate top-two WR tandem for the first time in years. WR need drops from critical to depth-only. Some WR3 depth may still be added via draft.

signings.contract_confidence: high — confirmed by OTC, Spotrac, ESPN, NFL Network
signings.analysis: 3yr/$36.75M is the second-largest external FA deal in Steelers history. Dean is the clear CB2 starter opposite Joey Porter Jr. Three interceptions and 9 PDs in 14 starts in 2025. $12M guaranteed is modest but 2026 cap hit is just $5.5M due to structure, limiting risk. Health history (missed 21 games in 7 seasons) is a concern, but the deal is structured for easy outs. CB goes from a real need to well-addressed. Combined with re-signed Samuel Jr., cornerback room is now a strength.

signings.contract_confidence: high — confirmed by NFL Network, Aaron Wilson/KPRC, Spotrac
signings.notes: Exact guaranteed money not yet publicly reported; estimated at ~$6M based on AAV
signings.analysis: 2yr/$12.25M is solid starter-tier money at RB. Dowdle had back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons (1,079 with DAL in 2024, 1,076 with CAR in 2025). Pure power runner who reunites with HC McCarthy. However, he doesn't fully replicate Gainwell's receiving-back role (95 career receptions vs Gainwell's pass-catching prowess). Jaylen Warren and Kaleb Johnson round out the room. RB drops from elevated need to reduced need but room still lacks a true pass-catching RB.

signings.contract_confidence: high — confirmed by Rapoport, Garafolo, ESPN, NFL.com
signings.analysis: One-year deal is a prove-it structure driven by Brisker's significant concussion history (3 in first 3 NFL seasons). Despite this, Brisker is expected to start and was one of the best available safeties at huge value ($5.5M). He played all 17 games in 2025, his first full season, posting 93 tackles, 1 INT, 8 PDs. Can play box or free safety. Combined with Darnell Savage (depth) and existing DeShon Elliott/Jalen Ramsey, the safety group is substantially improved. However, the 1-year structure means S remains a long-term need for the draft. One more concussion likely ends his career.

signings.contract_confidence: high — confirmed by Post-Gazette (Dulac), Rapoport, Spotrac
signings.notes: Extension adds 1 year to existing deal; total two-year deal through 2027 worth $32.25M with $16.25M fully guaranteed. Also frees ~$5.5M in 2026 cap space via restructure.
signings.analysis: 2nd-team All-Pro in 2025 (3.5 sacks, 78 tackles, 1 FF). Extension locks him through 2027 with first year fully guaranteed. IDL was not a critical need given Heyward's presence; this retention confirms it stays a non-need at the top. Depth behind Heyward (Benton, Harmon, Otomewo, Black) is adequate for now.

Low-risk retention at $4M/1yr. Samuel only joined mid-2025 after spinal fusion surgery and started 3 of 6 games. Still a reclamation project at 26 years old. Adds CB room depth alongside Porter Jr. and Dean. Not a starter-level commitment — this is continuity/depth, not a need-solver. Dean is the real CB need answer.

signings.contract_confidence: medium — reported by Ari Meirov/MySportsUpdate; official confirmation pending
signings.notes: Guaranteed money not yet publicly confirmed; estimates TBD based on structure
signings.analysis: LB3 re-signing behind Patrick Queen and Payton Wilson. 2yr/$5M is pure depth money. Holcomb's value is as a special teamer and quality rotation piece, not a starter. LB was not a pressing need and this does not move the needle on that positional grade.

Veteran minimum deal for a former 2019 first-round pick. Has started 83 of 97 career games and played both S spots. Purely a depth/camp signing. Per analysts, no guarantee he makes the 53-man roster. Adds veteran insurance behind Brisker and Elliott but doesn't address the long-term safety need.

signings.contract_confidence: medium — confirmed by Pelissero/official transactions page; dollar value not publicly disclosed
signings.notes: Contract value not yet officially reported. Low-cost rotational deal expected based on prior 1yr deal structure.
signings.analysis: Re-signed rotational DL who played 173 snaps across 12 games in 2025 with 9 tackles and 1 sack. Quality insurance behind Heyward, Benton, and Harmon. IDL was not a need and this doesn't change that picture.

signings.contract_confidence: high — confirmed by official Steelers transactions page
signings.notes: Financial terms not reported; likely veteran minimum or near-minimum
signings.analysis: Former 4th-round pick with 67 career games but didn't appear in a game for Pittsburgh in 2025 after signing to the practice squad. A swing OL option who can play G or T. Does not address the starting LG vacancy left by Seumalo.

signings.contract_confidence: high — confirmed by CBS Pittsburgh, ESPN tracker
signings.notes: Financial terms not reported; prior deal with PIT in 2024 was 3yr/$9M but this is a new 1-year deal
signings.analysis: Direct replacement for departed Corliss Waitman. Johnston was the team's designated punter in 2024 before a Week 1 injury. Averaged 44.0 yds/punt before his 2025 injury with Buffalo. Familiar with the organization. Punter need fully resolved.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Pittman trade + 3yr/$59M extension pairs a proven 80-catch WR alongside DK Metcalf. WR is no longer a critical need. Some WR3 depth still needed (Calvin Austin/Thielen departed) and the team acknowledges this, but it's a depth pick not an early-round investment.

Dean signing (3yr/$36.75M, $12M gtd) slots in as CB2 starter opposite Joey Porter Jr. Re-signing of Samuel Jr. provides a third option. CB room is now a projected strength with Porter Jr., Dean, Samuel, and Jalen Ramsey in the mix. No early-round CB necessary.

Gainwell loss was significant but Dowdle (2yr/$12.25M) is a legitimate starter with back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons. Jaylen Warren and Kaleb Johnson add further depth. However, Dowdle is a power runner and the receiving-back void from Gainwell is not fully addressed. RB is reduced from elevated to a modest depth consideration in middle rounds.

Brisker (1yr/$5.5M) is a legitimate starter despite the prove-it structure, and Savage adds depth. DeShon Elliott and Jalen Ramsey (transitioning to safety) provide further coverage. The safety group is substantially improved, but Brisker's concussion history and one-year deal mean long-term safety depth via mid-round draft pick remains advisable.

Seumalo's departure to Arizona (3yr/$31.5M) creates a genuine starter void at LG with no established replacement. Spencer Anderson is the best in-house option but is unproven as a full-time starter. Driscoll is depth only. IOL/LG becomes the top remaining need heading into the draft — a priority in rounds 1-3.

Aaron Rodgers (42) remains uncommitted and unsigned as of March 13. The team missed out on Kyler Murray (went to Vikings). Mason Rudolph and Will Howard are the only QBs on the roster. This is the biggest positional question mark on the entire roster. Need unchanged — either Rodgers returns or this becomes a Day 1/2 draft priority.

Heyward extension (2yr/$32.25M, $16.25M gtd) locks in the starter through 2027. Benton, Harmon, and Otomewo provide solid depth. Loudermilk, Lowry, and Ekuale departed but are replaceable depth. IDL is not a premium draft priority.

Jonnu Smith released, Connor Heyward departed to Raiders. Pat Freiermuth and Darnell Washington remain as the starting TE duo. The starter tandem is intact and capable, but depth/FB hybrid role is now thin. TE/FB depth worth addressing in Day 3.

Broderick Jones is recovering from spinal fusion surgery with training camp status unclear. No offensive tackle moves have been made. OT remains a meaningful need heading into the draft. No FA movement to change this tier.
Queen + Wilson + Holcomb (re-signed) is a complete room. LB is not a need. Note: Queen's name has been floated in trade rumors — if he is dealt, this immediately escalates to Tier 2.

key_additions.headline: Legitimate WR2 finally arrives — 80 catches and 7 TDs in 2025, locks in alongside DK Metcalf on 3-year deal
Second-biggest external FA deal in team history fills CB2 void opposite Joey Porter Jr.
Pittsburgh native returns home on high-value prove-it deal to anchor an overhauled secondary
Back-to-back 1,000-yard rusher and McCarthy reunion pick fills Gainwell's vacancy
2nd-team All-Pro DT retained through 2027; historically largest deal ever for an NFL defender age 36+

key_losses.headline: Three-year starting LG departs, leaving a critical void at left guard with no proven replacement
Team's leading all-purpose back (1,023 scrimmage yards, 8 TDs in 2025) leaves for Tampa Bay
Cap casualty after disastrous lone season (5.8 yds/rec); Freiermuth and D. Washington reassume TE lead
Four-year Pittsburgh staple and Cam's brother exits; FB/TE hybrid role now unfilled
Solid CB who stepped into starting role in 2025 departs; Dean and Samuel Jr. signings absorb the loss

notes: Aaron Rodgers remains unsigned as of 2026-03-13. His return to Pittsburgh is widely assumed but not confirmed. QB need remains Tier 1.
Guaranteed money for Rico Dowdle not officially confirmed; estimated at ~$6M based on comparable deals and AAV.
Cole Holcomb re-signing reported by Ari Meirov but not yet on official team transactions page; listed as 'expected' — included as confirmed based on multiple corroborating sources.
Esezi Otomewo and Jack Driscoll financial terms not publicly disclosed; contract values entered as 0 pending disclosure.
Cameron Johnston punter deal financial terms not yet officially reported.
Cap space post-FA estimated at ~$14M based on early free agency reporting; actual figure will shift as further transactions process and before roster bonuses clear.
Patrick Queen trade rumors active as of research date — if traded, LB escalates to Tier 2 and ~$17M cap space is added.
Broderick Jones (OT) recovering from spinal fusion surgery; status for training camp is uncertain per Still Curtain reporting.
Steelers hold 12 draft picks in 2026 including 4 compensatory selections (3rd, 4th, two 6ths).
